rules.description: Use formal addressing with third person pronouns, never use 'você' or informal treatment 'tu'
rules.notes: The target audience is businesses, so the style should be appropriate for business

rules.description: In situations where text can be aimed at both males and females, use the non-marked form (masculine)
rules.notes: Whenever possible and when it doesn't affect quality, choose a more neutral option

rules.description: Register should be professional and welcoming, business casual
rules.notes: Be precise and clear, consistent and natural. Avoid unnecessarily formal or pompous tone

rules.description: Avoid condescending, childish or overly familiar tone and treat the reader as an adult and professional
rules.notes: The tone of American documentation is often paternalistic. Strive for clarity without unnecessary repetition

rules.description: Do not use 'Por favor' excessively. Be more direct and use the imperative form where appropriate
rules.example_correct: Introduza o seu nome de utilizador.
rules.example_incorrect: Por favor, introduza o seu nome de utilizador.
rules.notes: Portuguese tends not to use 'Por favor' as much as English uses 'Please'

rules.description: Use gender-neutral writing when possible, but when text can be aimed at both males and females, use the non-marked form (masculine)
rules.example_correct: A equipa de vendas entrará em contacto com o cliente para lhe fornecer informações.
rules.example_incorrect: A equipa de vendas entrará em contacto com a/o cliente para lhe fornecer informações.

rules.description: Avoid passive voice whenever possible, use active form to improve readability
rules.example_correct: O software guarda todos os ficheiros no diretório.
rules.example_incorrect: Todos os ficheiros são guardados no diretório pelo software.
rules.notes: Passive voice is only advised in specific texts like legal and official documents

rules.description: Avoid extensive use of possessive adjectives. Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives
rules.example_correct: Pode editar os ficheiros num computador ou dispositivo móvel.
rules.example_incorrect: Pode editar os seus ficheiros no seu computador ou no seu dispositivo móvel.
rules.notes: In some cases, 'our' refers to a subject in the third person

rules.description: Translate –ing form appropriately based on context (verb, noun, infinitive clause, subordinate clause, etc.)
rules.example_correct: Os nossos controladores têm uma cache que protege a integridade dos dados

rules.description: Avoid unnecessary words, concentrate on meaning and render it as clearly as possible
rules.example_correct: Nome, endereço de e-mail e telefone
rules.example_incorrect: O seu nome, o seu endereço de e-mail, o seu telefone
rules.notes: English sometimes uses additional words to express concepts

rules.description: Avoid overtranslation - the use of unnecessary words where a less verbose version would be equally clear
Use pronouns or other techniques to avoid repetitions that are acceptable in English but not in Portuguese
rules.example_correct: Para eliminar um ficheiro do computador, selecione-o no painel direito e clique em Eliminar.
rules.example_incorrect: Para eliminar um ficheiro do computador, selecione o ficheiro no painel direito e clique em Eliminar.

rules.description: Be careful with false friends and literal translations that can have different meanings
rules.notes: For English 'actually'

rules.description: Translate 'support' correctly
Translate 'comprehensive' correctly
Translate 'particular' correctly
Translate 'proper' correctly
Translate buttons as verbs, generally using the infinitive form
Be concise in button translations, avoid using articles or prepositions
Button translations do not end in a period, unless they consist of more than one sentence
Capitalize the first word of the button
Do NOT use quotation marks around buttons even if the English source uses them
rules.example_correct: Clique no botão Guardar e fechar, abaixo.
rules.example_incorrect: Clique no botão "Guardar e fechar" abaixo.

rules.description: Replace English examples with Portuguese equivalents when needed
rules.example_correct: Por exemplo, pesquisar '*este' selecionará itens que contenham 'nordeste', 'sudeste', 'este' ou 'oeste'. Pesquisar 's?lva' devolverá itens como 'silva' e 'salva'.
rules.notes: Adapt examples to local context while maintaining functionality

rules.description: Use lowercase for nationalities, languages, religions, days of the week, months, season names, currencies and job titles
Use lowercase for months
Use lowercase for currencies
Use lowercase for languages
Use lowercase for nationalities
Use Title Case for proper names, geographical names, organizations, bodies, departments, awards, events, and product names
rules.notes: Follow source capitalization for product names

rules.description: Use Sentence case in titles, headings, UI options - only capitalize the first word
Exception for legal texts: retain capitalization that conveys specific meaning
rules.example_correct: O presente acordo (o "Acordo") é um acordo vinculativo
rules.example_incorrect: O presente acordo (o "acordo") é um acordo vinculativo
rules.notes: Legal implications require preserving original capitalization

rules.description: Preserve all uppercase formatting in legal paragraphs and include accentuation on uppercase letters
rules.notes: These conventions may have legal implications

rules.description: Brand name tags need to be separated from preceding or following text with a space
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in Portuguese
Use non-breaking spaces to prevent separation of people's names, numbers, software names and versions at line ends
rules.notes: Use Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or Alt+0160

rules.description: Use comma with vocatives and interjections
Use comma with apposition
Use comma with subordinate clauses and conditional clauses
rules.example_correct: Quando for necessário, use a vírgula. Se necessário, use a vírgula.

rules.description: Use comma when an object or simple word/expression starts the sentence before the subject
Use comma with interspersed clauses
rules.example_correct: Os casos em que, como referido acima, deve usar a vírgula.

rules.description: Use comma with reduced clauses of infinitive, participle, omitted verb
rules.example_correct: Ao entrar, clique aqui. Fechada a janela, ele sentou-se. Ele é, eu não.

rules.description: Use comma before 'mas' and before and after 'porém', 'todavia', 'contudo'
rules.notes: For adversatives

rules.description: Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in a table, or after captions unless the expression is a full sentence
Do not use full stops if a sentence ends with an abbreviation, interrogation or exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
When a sentence is placed between brackets or quotes, the period is placed outside
Use colons only to introduce lists or before quotations. Give preference to commas or periods elsewhere
rules.example_correct: Abra o assistente Criar um módulo e clique no separador Pacote.
rules.example_incorrect: Abra o assistente Criar um módulo: clique no separador Pacote.

rules.description: If a colon is followed by more text, the first word after the colon is not capitalized unless it starts in a new line or is a mandatory Title/Sentence case term
Avoid semicolons. Start a new sentence instead
rules.example_correct: As categorias incluem: computadores, portáteis e dispositivos móveis, assim como rede, routers e firewalls.
rules.example_incorrect: As categorias incluem: computadores, portáteis e dispositivos móveis; rede, routers e firewalls.

rules.description: For marketing and PR content, semicolons should not be used. Cut the sentence in two smaller ones to enhance readability
Semicolons should mainly be used to separate items in an enumeration when using only commas would be confusing
Use question marks with caution due to Portuguese formality. In titles, prefer affirmative sentences
Replace questions with affirmative statements in titles when appropriate
Use exclamation marks with caution due to Portuguese formality. Sometimes better to remove them
When multiple exclamation marks appear in groups, keep only one
Use hyphen in compound words, to split words and as negative sign
Use n dash in ranges of numbers and letters, preferably without surrounding spaces
Use m dash for stronger emphasis, surrounded by blank spaces
rules.notes: Can sometimes be changed to brackets or colon

rules.description: Use round brackets for further explanations, spelling out acronyms, indicating singular/plural forms, or for translations of UI items
rules.example_correct: Introduza o endereço (UNC ou URL) e clique em Validar.

rules.description: Use square brackets for indicating variables or additional information within round brackets
rules.example_correct: Especifique o caminho (UNC [interno] ou URL [externo]) e clique em Guardar.

rules.description: Always use curly double quotes, even if the source has single quotes
rules.notes: When quotation marks are inconsistent in source, use curly quotation marks in Portuguese

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
Use straight apostrophes consistently instead of curly apostrophes
If bulleted items are complete sentences with a conjugated verb, each begins with a capital and ends with a period
rules.example_correct:
Os seguintes casos são possíveis:
● O servidor está na rede.
● O servidor está fora da rede.

rules.description: If enumeration parts consist of a single word or small group of words, punctuation marks may be omitted
rules.example_correct:
O servidor pode estar nas seguintes localizações:
● dentro da rede
● fora da rede

rules.description: If list items are not full sentences and depend syntactically on the introductory sentence, start with lowercase, end with semicolon, and end the last item with a period
Avoid abbreviations whenever possible, except for standard abbreviations or those in RingCentral glossary
Be consistent - do not abbreviate a term in some sentences and spell it out in others
Articles and prepositions can be omitted in on-screen text only in exceptional situations due to space constraints
rules.notes: Do not use 'telegram style' if not necessary

rules.description: For first appearance of acronyms, spell out full term with acronym in brackets
rules.notes: After initial clarification, acronym can be used alone

rules.description: If there is no translation for acronym, keep the spelled out term in brackets after the acronym
If there is a translation, insert full version and acronym in brackets
For acronyms not spelled out, use acronym only if it requires no explanation or insert brief explanation first time it appears
rules.notes: Afterwards, use only the acronym

rules.description: Write numbers in full when used as nouns
Write numbers in full at the beginning of a sentence
Write numbers 1-10 in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities
Write dates, large numbers, ages, document references as numbers
Numbers 1 to 9999 should be written without spacing. For numbers above, separate with non-breaking spaces
Replace decimal point with comma and thousand separator with non-breaking space
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Units of measure should be separated from the preceding number by a non-breaking space
Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches as an exception
Unit symbols are not abbreviations and should never be followed by a dot
Write percentages in numbers followed by % symbol with a non-breaking space
Use short date format DD/MM/YYYY separated by slash
rules.notes: Can also use hyphen separation less frequently

rules.description: Use long date format: dddd d mmmm yyyy
Use 24-hour clock format: hh:mm:ss
Use 'das' and 'às' for time ranges
Currency symbol or code should always be written AFTER the number with non-breaking spaces
To distinguish between US and CA dollars, give preference to ISO abbreviation
Translate 'about' as 'sobre'
Translate 'as shown in figure xx' as 'como mostra a imagem xx'
Translate 'For more information on xxx, see xxx' as 'Para obter mais informações sobre xxx, consulte xxx'
rules.example_correct: Para obter mais informações sobre xxx, consulte xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'N/A (Non Available)' as 'N/D (não disponível)'
Translate 'Please note that' as 'Note que'
Translate 'Refer to xxx for instructions on xxx' as 'Consulte xxx para obter instruções sobre xxx'
rules.example_correct: Consulte xxx para obter instruções sobre xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'registered trademark' as 'marca registada'
Translate 'trademark' as 'marca comercial'
Translate 'troubleshooting' as 'resolução de problemas'
Translate 'User's Guide' as 'Manual do utilizador'
Translate 'Are you sure you want to...?' as 'Tem a certeza de que pretende…?'
rules.example_correct: Tem a certeza de que pretende…?

rules.description: Translate 'Contact [support]' as 'Contacte [support]'
Translate 'Follow these steps to xxx' as 'Para xxx, siga estes passos:'
Application/product names are often trademarked and rarely translated. Verify translatability before translating any application, product, or feature name
Use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on first mention in translated content unless otherwise indicated

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: Complete extensive research to determine if an official, approved trademarked translated version is available for third party trademarks

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: For third party product names, follow the localized equivalence in official websites if available
Use official approved translation for copyright notices if available
rules.example_correct: ©2015 RingCentral, Inc. Todos os direitos reservados. RingCentral, RingCentral Office, RingCentral Meetings e o logótipo RingCentral são marcas comerciais registadas da RingCentral, Inc. Outras marcas e logótipos de terceiros mencionados neste documento são marcas comerciais dos respetivos proprietários.
rules.notes: If no official translation is available, use the provided Portuguese version

rules.description: Query if URLs have local versions and modify accordingly. If not, leave as in source English text
If software has been translated, match the relevant translation memory or string list provided
If software has not been translated, use bilingual format: English first, followed by target in brackets
rules.notes: Insert translation outside of formatting tags when there are formatting tags

rules.description: Translate country and city names based on Portuguese conventions
Localize address formats based on Portuguese conventions if necessary
If source includes only toll free and US numbers, add international country code to local number for international users
RingCentral Glossary is the first and binding source for technical terms
For terms not in glossary, stick to Microsoft standard terminology where applicable
Use available online resources like Wikipedia, administrator web pages, large IT vendor portals for technical terms
rules.notes: Be careful of differences between Portuguese and Brazilian Portuguese

rules.description: For internal cross-references, reference consistently throughout translation and all files
rules.notes: References to chapters, sections, headings within the document being translated

rules.description: For external cross-references, check if text/document has been localized before and match relevant translations
rules.notes: If document has not been translated before, leave as in source language
